items.title: EEG biomarkers of reinforcement learning and motivation: A multi-task battery
items.abstract: Serotonin and dopamine make dissociable contributions to reinforcement learning (RL) sub-components, yet we lack neural biomarkers capable of detecting their differential effects. Here, we report the development of a five-task EEG battery designed to probe these dissociable RL mechanisms. Using a model-free analytical approach in healthy volunteers, we identify distinct neural markers across probabilistic instrumental learning, motivational vigour, Pavlovian-instrumental transfer, reversal learning, and a working memory-RL task. A centro-parietal P300 tracked incremental learning across three paradigms and showed cross-task convergent validity. Readiness potentials and beta suppression indexed value-based motor preparation, while frontal theta captured Pavlovian-instrumental conflict. The largely independent pattern across markers supports the battery's capacity to detect selective pharmacological effects on distinct neural systems.
items.llm_evidence_en: develops EEG biomarkers for reinforcement learning and motivation
items.llm_evidence_cn: 开发用于强化学习和动机的脑电生物标志物

items.llm_tldr_en: This paper reports a five-task EEG battery to identify neural biomarkers for dissociable reinforcement learning sub-components (probabilistic learning, motivation, Pavlovian-instrumental transfer, reversal learning, working memory-RL).
items.llm_tldr_cn: 本文报告了一个五任务脑电（EEG）电池组，用于识别分离性强化学习子成分（概率性工具学习、动机活力、巴甫洛夫-工具转移、逆转学习和工作记忆-强化学习）的神经标志物。在健康志愿者中采用无模型分析方法，发现中央顶叶P300追踪跨范式增量学习，准备电位和β抑制指数价值相关的运动准备，额叶θ与认知控制相关。该电池组提供了检测5-羟色胺和多巴胺差异效应的潜在工具。
items.llm_tldr: 本文报告了一个五任务脑电（EEG）电池组，用于识别分离性强化学习子成分（概率性工具学习、动机活力、巴甫洛夫-工具转移、逆转学习和工作记忆-强化学习）的神经标志物。在健康志愿者中采用无模型分析方法，发现中央顶叶P300追踪跨范式增量学习，准备电位和β抑制指数价值相关的运动准备，额叶θ与认知控制相关。该电池组提供了检测5-羟色胺和多巴胺差异效应的潜在工具。

items.title: Partitioning away consciousness: an equal and cross-frequency connectivity analysis from the integration-segregation perspective
items.abstract: Derived from previous observations on equal and cross-frequency coupling, we evaluated the proposal that equal and cross-frequency phase synchronization may characterize the integration-segregation perspective of cerebral sensory-motor processing. Using brain recordings obtained in normal conditions and in conditions of diminished sensory input (eyes closed wakefulness, sleep and coma, when there is presumably less functional segregation of sensory-motor processing in neural networks), we assessed potential differences in partitioning of the synchrony state space linked to cross-frequency synchronization. More partitions were found in conditions of decreased sensory input. In addition, there was a less complex synchrony state space in cross-frequency as compared with equal-frequency coupling, in terms of fewer connectivity configurations. These results support the idea that equal-frequency coupling favours integration from multiple brain regions occurring in a complex synchrony state space rich in possible connectivity configurations, whereas cross-frequency coupling contributes to segregation, or localized sensory-motor transformations taking place in specific brain areas. This evidence may contribute to new considerations about the much-discussed role of multi-frequency relations in neuronal activity, and how the structural and functional modular organization of the nervous system is able to generate the coordinated activity needed for consciousness and appropriate cognitive behaviors in complex environments.
items.llm_evidence_en: EEG connectivity analysis in sleep and coma states
items.llm_evidence_cn: 睡眠和昏迷状态下的EEG连接分析
items.llm_evidence: 睡眠和昏迷状态下的EEG连接分析
items.canonical_evidence: 睡眠和昏迷状态下的EEG连接分析
items.llm_tldr_en: Analyzes equal and cross-frequency phase synchronization in EEG during sleep, coma, and eyes-closed wakefulness from an integration-segregation perspective.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文从整合-分离视角出发，分析正常状态与感官输入减少状态（闭眼、睡眠、昏迷）下脑电的等频和跨频相位同步。发现感官输入减少时，同步状态空间的划分增多，提示功能分离减弱。该研究加深了对意识状态和睡眠中脑功能组织的理解。
items.llm_tldr: 该论文从整合-分离视角出发，分析正常状态与感官输入减少状态（闭眼、睡眠、昏迷）下脑电的等频和跨频相位同步。发现感官输入减少时，同步状态空间的划分增多，提示功能分离减弱。该研究加深了对意识状态和睡眠中脑功能组织的理解。
